title: polygala welwitschii subsp. pygmaea
intro: Polygala welwitschii subsp. pygmaea is a subspecies of the Polygala welwitschii plant, a flowering plant in the milkwort family (Polygalaceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in African and Asian cultures, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Polygala welwitschii subsp. pygmaea include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as being used to lower blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of diabetes. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional remedies such as poultices and salves.
